warmups.name: Lateral Lunge Weight Shifts
warmups.desc:
Targets: Hip Adductors & Glutes. Biomechanics: Gently shift weight side-to-side to lubricate joints. - - Stand wide, push hips back, shift weight left to right.
- Cue: "Imagine sliding side-to-side on a slippery floor."

warmups.name: Dynamic Leg Swings
warmups.desc:
Targets: Hip Flexors & Extensors. Biomechanics: Lubricates the entire hip capsule. - - Swing one leg forward and back like a pendulum.
- Cue: "Feel the stretch at the top of the forward and backward swing."

compound.name: Side Lunge
compound.desc:
Targets: Gluteus Medius (Primary), Adductors (Primary), Quads (Secondary). - - Step to the side, bend the working knee, keep trailing leg straight.
- Cue: "Push the floor away with your bent leg to return to center."

isolations.name: Cable Hip Abduction
isolations.desc:
Targets: Gluteus Medius. Biomechanics: Constant resistance against hip abduction. - - Stand sideways to a low cable. Move your leg away from your body's midline.
- Cue: "Squeeze from the outside of your hips."

isolations.name: Cable Adduction
isolations.desc:
Targets: Adductor Group. Biomechanics: Constant cable tension pulling across the midline. - - Stand sideways to a low cable. Pull the cabled leg across your standing leg.
- Cue: "Imagine squeezing a ball between your thighs."

cooldowns.name: Glute Bridge Pulses & Butterfly Stretch
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Glutes & Adductors. Biomechanics: Low-intensity active glute bridge pulses act as a muscle pump to flush lower body waste, followed by a static stretch to restore adductor resting length. - - Perform 15 slow, bodyweight glute bridges to flush blood through hips. Then, sit with the soles of your feet together and let knees fall wide.
- Cue: "Pulse gently to pump the muscles, then breathe deep and melt into the static butterfly stretch."

cooldowns.name: Kneeling Quad Stretch
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Quads & Hip Flexors. Biomechanics: Passive static hip extension to restore resting length of the rectus femoris. - - Kneel on one knee, pull back ankle to glute.
- Cue: "Tuck your tailbone under for a deeper stretch."

warmups.name: Arm Circles
warmups.desc:
Targets: Deltoid Complex. - - Make circles with arms extended.
- Cue: "Draw perfect circles with your fingertips."

warmups.name: Prone Y-Raises (Bodyweight)
warmups.desc:
Targets: Lower Trapezius & Rotator Cuff. Biomechanics: Active shoulder flexion and external rotation to secure the shoulder socket. - - Lie face down, extend arms in a "Y" shape, thumbs up. Lift arms.
- Cue: "Initiate the lift by pulling your shoulder blades down and together."

compound.name: 30-Degree Incline DB Press
compound.desc:
Targets: Upper Chest (Primary), Anterior Delt (Secondary), Triceps (Secondary). - - Press dumbbells up from a 30-degree incline.
- Cue: "Drive your biceps towards each other at the top."

isolations.name: Low-to-High Cable Crossover
isolations.desc:
Targets: Upper Chest. Biomechanics: Constant upward/inward arc tension. - - Pull low cables upward and together to shoulder height.
- Cue: "Scoop the air up and in."

isolations.name: Cable Front Raise
isolations.desc:
Targets: Anterior Deltoid. Biomechanics: Constant cable tension in shoulder flexion. - - Stand facing away from a low cable, pull straight up in front of you.
- Cue: "Lift the weight using the front of your shoulder, keeping arms straight."

isolations.name: Overhead Cable Triceps Ext.
isolations.desc:
Targets: Triceps (Long Head). Biomechanics: Constant tension in a stretched position. - - Face away from high cable, extend elbows overhead.
- Cue: "Pin elbows to your head; only move forearms."

cooldowns.name: Arm Hug Flutters & Doorway Chest Stretch
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Pectorals & Anterior Deltoids. Biomechanics: Gentle, dynamic arm crosses pump lactic acid out of the upper chest, followed by a static stretch to open up tight fibers. - - Slowly cross arms over your chest, alternating top arms for 1 minute. Then, place forearms on a doorframe and gently step forward.
- Cue: "Hug yourself slowly to flush the chest, then lean forward to statically stretch the pecs."

cooldowns.name: Overhead Triceps Stretch
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Triceps & Lats. Biomechanics: Passive elbow flexion and shoulder elevation to release the triceps. - - Arm overhead, bend elbow, pull with opposite hand.
- Cue: "Reach your hand down your spine."

warmups.name: Bodyweight Forward Lunges
warmups.desc:
Targets: Quads & Gluteus Maximus. - - Step forward, lower hips to 90 degrees.
- Cue: "Keep front knee aligned with second toe."

warmups.name: Bodyweight Clamshells
warmups.desc:
Targets: Gluteus Medius. Biomechanics: Direct lateral hip external rotation to prime pelvic stabilizers and prevent knee valgus. - - Lie on side, knees bent. Keep feet together and lift top knee.
- Cue: "Imagine hips stacked against a wall; open knee using only the side glute."

compound.name: Walking Lunge
compound.desc:
Targets: Quads (Primary), Gluteus Max (Primary), Hamstrings (Secondary). - - Step forward, lower hips, push off rear foot.
- Cue: "Fall forward with control."

isolations.name: Seated Leg Curl
isolations.desc:
Targets: Hamstrings. Biomechanics: Hip flexion pre-stretches the hamstrings for superior ROM. - - Sit in machine, curl pad towards you one leg at a time.
- Cue: "Squeeze the hamstring to move the weight, controlling the release."

isolations.name: One-Leg Seated Calf Raise
isolations.desc:
Targets: Soleus. Biomechanics: Performed with a bent knee to isolate the soleus, which is crucial for ankle stability during deep knee flexion like in a lunge. - - Sit with foot on a raised surface, place weight on knee, raise heel.
- Cue: "Push through the ball of your foot."

cooldowns.name: Ankle Pumps & Seated Toe Touch
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Hamstrings & Calves. Biomechanics: Active ankle flexion/extension (pumping) promotes venous return to clear pooled blood, followed by a hamstring stretch. - - Sit with legs straight. Pump your ankles forward and back 20 times to clear lower leg waste, then hinge forward at the hips to reach for toes.
- Cue: "Pump the ankles to drain the legs, then melt into the hamstring fold."

cooldowns.name: Pigeon Pose
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Glutes & Hip Flexors. Biomechanics: Deep passive external rotation of the hip joint, providing a superior stretch for the gluteus maximus and medius. - - From plank, bring knee to outside of hand, stretch back leg.
- Cue: "Try to square your hips to the front."

warmups.name: Cat-Cow
warmups.desc:
Targets: Erector Spinae & Core. - - On all fours, arch and round back.
- Cue: "Initiate movement from your tailbone."

warmups.name: Bent-Over T-Raises (Unweighted)
warmups.desc:
Targets: Rhomboids & Mid-Traps. Biomechanics: Pure scapular retraction to prime upper back before pulling heavy weight. - - Hinge at hips, flat back. Raise straight arms to sides (making a "T").
- Cue: "Pinch a coin between your shoulder blades at the top."

compound.name: Bent-Over DB Row
compound.desc:
Targets: Lats (Primary), Biceps (Secondary), Rear Delts (Secondary). - - Hinge at hips, pull DBs to ribcage.
- Cue: "Pull your elbows into your back pockets."

isolations.name: Straight Arm Cable Pulldown
isolations.desc:
Targets: Latissimus Dorsi. Biomechanics: Constant tension matching shoulder extension. - - Stand, push straight bar down in an arc to thighs.
- Cue: "Act as a human protractor, hinge only at the shoulder."

isolations.name: Unilateral Incline Cable Bicep Curl
isolations.desc:
Targets: Biceps Brachii (Long Head). Biomechanics: Shoulder hyperextension provides maximum stretch and cable provides constant tension. - - Set bench to 45-60 degrees facing away from low cable, curl single handle.
- Cue: "Keep elbow pinned back to isolate the stretched bicep."

isolations.name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
isolations.desc:
Targets: Posterior Deltoid. Biomechanics: Constant tension in horizontal abduction. - - Cross arms at high cables, pull outward/backward.
- Cue: "Stretch a giant rubber band across your chest."

cooldowns.name: Wall Slide Mimics & Child's Pose
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Lats & Scapular Stabilizers. Biomechanics: Gentle active arm raises against a wall flush the mid-back and biceps, followed by spinal decompression in Child's Pose. - - Perform 10 slow bodyweight wall slides (arms sliding up/down wall) to pump the lats. Then, kneel, sit back on your heels, and stretch arms forward.
- Cue: "Flush the upper back with wall slides, then decompress and breathe into Child's Pose."

cooldowns.name: Cross-Body Shoulder Stretch
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Posterior Deltoid & Rhomboids. Biomechanics: Passive horizontal adduction to release the rear shoulder. - - Pull one arm across chest.
- Cue: "Look over the shoulder you are stretching."

warmups.name: Glute Bridges
warmups.desc:
Targets: Gluteus Maximus. - - Lie on back, lift hips.
- Cue: "Drive through your heels."

warmups.name: Fire Hydrants
warmups.desc:
Targets: Gluteus Medius. - - On all fours, lift knee to the side.
- Cue: "Don't spill the glass of water on your lower back."

compound.name: Curtsy Lunge
compound.desc:
Targets: Gluteus Medius (Primary), Glute Max (Primary), Adductors (Secondary). - - Step diagonally behind, bend both knees.
- Cue: "Push off the front foot to return to start."

isolations.name: Cable Hip Abduction
isolations.desc:
Targets: Gluteus Medius. Biomechanics: Constant resistance against hip abduction. - - Stand sideways to a low cable. Move your leg away from your body's midline.
- Cue: "Squeeze from the outside of your hips."

isolations.name: Cable Adduction
isolations.desc:
Targets: Adductors. Biomechanics: Constant machine tension pulling knees inward. - - Stand sideways to a low cable. Pull the cabled leg across your standing leg.
- Cue: "Drive your inner thighs together forcefully."

cooldowns.name: Quadruped Hip Rocks & Figure-4 Stretch
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Glutes, Piriformis, & Hips. Biomechanics: Controlled active rock-backs pump blood through the deep hip rotators, followed by a targeted static stretch. - - On hands and knees, slowly rock your hips back to your heels 10 times. Then, lie on your back, cross ankle over knee, and pull to chest.
- Cue: "Rock slowly to flush the hips, then hold the Figure-4 stretch to release the glutes."

cooldowns.name: Pigeon Pose
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Glutes & Hip Flexors. Biomechanics: Deep passive external rotation of the hip joint. - - From plank, bring knee to outside of hand, stretch back leg.
- Cue: "Try to square your hips to the front."

warmups.name: Scapular Wall Push-ups
warmups.desc:
Targets: Serratus Anterior. Biomechanics: Scapular protraction/upward rotation to ensure healthy tracking overhead. - - Hands flat on wall. Let chest sink in, then push away by spreading shoulder blades wide.
- Cue: "Push the wall away to hollow out your chest."

warmups.name: Wall Angels
warmups.desc:
Targets: Rotator Cuff. - - Back to wall, slide arms up/down.
- Cue: "Keep lower back flat against the wall."

compound.name: Standing Arnold Press
compound.desc:
Targets: Anterior Delt (Primary), Lateral Delt (Primary), Triceps (Secondary). - - Palms facing you, press and rotate so palms face away.
- Cue: "Uncork your shoulders as you press."

isolations.name: Cable Front Raise
isolations.desc:
Targets: Anterior Deltoid. Biomechanics: Constant tension through shoulder flexion. - - With low cable between legs or to the side, raise handle straight up.
- Cue: "Lift with the front of the shoulder, not momentum."

isolations.name: Leaning Cable Lateral Raise
isolations.desc:
Targets: Lateral Deltoid. Biomechanics: Leaning away from the machine increases the stretch at the bottom of the movement. - - Hold machine, lean away, raise handle out to the side.
- Cue: "Lead with the elbow and push the weight away from you."

isolations.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown
isolations.desc:
Targets: Triceps Brachii. Biomechanics: Constant downward tension isolating elbow extension. - - Pin elbows, push bar straight down.
- Cue: "Imagine your elbows are nailed to your ribs."

cooldowns.name: Shoulder Rolls & Behind-the-Back Stretch
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Deltoids & Upper Trapezius. Biomechanics: Active, slow shoulder rolls flush waste from the shoulders and neck, followed by a chest-opening chest stretch. - - Roll your shoulders slowly forward and backward for 1 minute. Then, clasp hands behind your back and gently lift your arms away from your body.
- Cue: "Roll the shoulders to clear tension, then clasp hands and open the chest."

cooldowns.name: Cross-Body Shoulder Stretch
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Posterior & Lateral Deltoids. Biomechanics: Passive horizontal adduction to release the rear and side shoulders, highly active in the Arnold Press. - - Pull one arm across chest.
- Cue: "Look over the shoulder you are stretching."

warmups.name: Bodyweight Good Mornings
warmups.desc:
Targets: Hamstrings & Erectors. - - Hinge at hips, keep back straight.
- Cue: "Push your butt to the wall behind you."

warmups.name: Leg Swings (Front-to-Back)
warmups.desc:
Targets: Hamstrings & Hip Flexors. - - Swing leg forward and back dynamically.
- Cue: "Feel the stretch at the end of each swing."

compound.name: Romanian Deadlift (RDL)
compound.desc:
Targets: Hamstrings (Primary), Glute Max (Primary), Erector Spinae (Secondary). - - Push hips backward, lower bar down legs.
- Cue: "Scrape your shins with the bar."

isolations.name: Seated Leg Curl
isolations.desc:
Targets: Hamstrings. Biomechanics: Hip flexion pre-stretches the hamstrings for superior ROM. - - Sit in machine, curl pad towards you one leg at a time.
- Cue: "Squeeze the hamstring to move the weight, controlling the release."

isolations.name: One-Leg Standing Calf Raise
isolations.desc:
Targets: Gastrocnemius. Biomechanics: Performed with a straight/locked knee to fully engage the gastrocnemius, which acts as a posterior stabilizer in straight-leg movements like the RDL. - - Stand on one leg, raise heel as high as possible.
- Cue: "Drive your body straight up to the ceiling."

cooldowns.name: Dynamic Hip Rocks & Downward Facing Dog
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Posterior Chain (Hamstrings, Glutes, Erectors). Biomechanics: Gentle active hip rocking on all fours flushes the lower back and hamstrings, followed by a full-body stretch. - - On all fours, gently rock hips side-to-side for 1 minute. Then, press your hips up and back into an inverted V-shape.
- Cue: "Rock the hips to flush the lower back, then push up into Downward Dog."

cooldowns.name: Supine Knee-to-Chest
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Glutes & Lower Back. Biomechanics: Passive spinal flexion to decompress lower back erectors. - - Lie on back, pull knee to chest.
- Cue: "Keep your tailbone on the floor."

warmups.name: Light Scapular Pulldowns
warmups.desc:
Targets: Lower Traps & Lats. - - Light weight, pull shoulder blades down/back without bending elbows.
- Cue: "Put your shoulder blades in your back pockets."

warmups.name: Dead Hang
warmups.desc:
Targets: Lats & Shoulder Capsule. Biomechanics: Passive overhead stretch to decompress the spine and open the shoulder joint. - - Hang from a pull-up bar with a relaxed shoulder girdle for 15-20 seconds.
- Cue: "Let gravity pull your hips down and stretch your lats."

compound.name: Lat Pulldown
compound.desc:
Targets: Latissimus Dorsi (Primary), Brachialis (Secondary), Biceps (Secondary). - - Wide grip, pull bar down to upper chest.
- Cue: "Drive your elbows down towards the floor."

isolations.name: Straight Arm Cable Pulldown
isolations.desc:
Targets: Latissimus Dorsi. Biomechanics: Constant cable tension isolating shoulder extension. - - Push straight bar down in an arc.
- Cue: "Hinge only at the shoulder."

isolations.name: Standing Unilateral Cable Hammer Curl
isolations.desc:
Targets: Brachialis & Brachioradialis. Biomechanics: Neutral grip places biceps at a disadvantage, isolating the brachialis. Standing allows full peak contraction. - - Use a single D-handle, keep palm facing inward, curl up.
- Cue: "Keep your wrist locked in a neutral hammer position."

isolations.name: Unilateral Incline Cable Bicep Curl
isolations.desc:
Targets: Biceps Brachii (Long Head). Biomechanics: Shoulder hyperextension provides maximum stretch and cable provides constant tension. - - Set bench to 45-60 degrees facing away from low cable, curl single handle.
- Cue: "Keep elbow pinned back to isolate the stretched bicep."

cooldowns.name: Active Hand Flips & Wrist Stretch
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Forearm Flexors, Extensors, & Lats. Biomechanics: Rhythmic finger and wrist movements pump lactic acid out of the forearms, followed by passive wrist stretches. - - Extend arms and rapidly open/close fists 20 times, rotating the wrists. Then, pull fingers back and down to stretch the forearms.
- Cue: "Pump the hands to clear forearm burning, then hold the wrist stretch."

cooldowns.name: Overhead Lat Stretch
cooldowns.desc:
Targets: Lats. Biomechanics: Passive lateral shoulder flexion to stretch the lats. - - Arm overhead, grasp wrist, pull gently to side.
- Cue: "Reach up before you reach over."
